name: write_file
description: Creates a new file OR fully rewrites an existing file with the complete content provided. The whole file is replaced byte-for-byte. PREFER `edit_file` over this tool when you are only changing parts of an existing file — write_file is for creation or wholesale rewrites (~70%+ of lines change). Returns `resolved_path` / `project_relative` / `input_given` metadata; if PATH_NORMALIZED is shown, use the resolved_path in subsequent tool calls. Writes are atomic (temp file + fsync + os.replace) with byte-for-byte read-back validation.

file_path.description: The path where the file should be created or fully rewritten. Project-relative or absolute inside the working directory.

content.description: The COMPLETE content to write. This replaces any existing content — for partial edits use edit_file instead to avoid the cost and risk of regenerating the entire file.

overwrite.description: Replace the file if it already exists. Defaults to true — the tool always writes the exact content you supply and validates it byte-for-byte after writing. Set to false only when you explicitly want to fail on collision.
